data.title: Major U.S. Stock Indexes Close Higher; Bond Yields Rise — WSJ
data.description:
By Rebecca Feng

Hollywood will never be quite the same.

Netflix has agreed to buy Warner Bros. Discovery's studios and streaming business for $83 billion, including debt, in a mammoth deal that is set to reshape the entertainment and media industry. Warner Bros. shares rallied 5%. Stock in Paramount Skydance, a rival suitor, sank 9.8% as investors awaited its next move and reaction from Washington.

Stock indexes pared early gains with the Nasdaq composite finishing up 0.3%. All three major U.S. benchmarks closed the week higher, with the S&P 500 within 0.5% of its record closing high.

The Federal Reserve got one last inflation reading to consider before next week's rate decision, at which it is widely expected to cut rates again.

A delayed readout of the Fed's preferred gauge, PCE, showed price pressures accelerating slightly in September. The personal-consumption expenditures index rose 2.8% from a year earlier, in line with consensus expectations.

Overseas, Asian benchmarks mostly rose, except for the Nikkei 225, which was buffeted by rising expectations for a Bank of Japan rate hike. The Stoxx Europe 600 closed nearly flat.

In recent trading:

  • Bond yields rose. The 10-year Treasury yield ended the week at 4.138% after its largest one-week gain since April.
  • Bitcoin prices dropped below $90,000.
  • A stronger U.K. pound weighed on the dollar. The WSJ Dollar Index is on track for its biggest two-week percentage decline since early July.
  • Copper futures rose in London, hitting another record closing high amid fears of new U.S. tariffs.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: El Salvador Inflation Rate Rises to 15-Month High
data.description:
Consumer prices in El Salvador rose 1.14% year-over-year in November 2025, up from 0.93% in October, marking the third consecutive month of inflation.

It remained the highest rate since August 2024.

The increase was led by food (and related items), which rose 2.28% (from 1.41% in October).

Additional upward pressure came from restaurants and hotels (3.48% vs 3.41%) and healthcare (2.22% vs 1.88%), while housing and utilities eased to 1.30% from 1.68%.

By contrast, prices continued to fall for transport (-2.86% vs -2.02%), clothing and footwear (-0.62% vs -0.72%), household goods (-0.12% vs -0.60%), and communications (-0.37% vs -0.36%).

On a monthly basis, consumer prices rose 0.11% in November, following a 0.28% increase in October.

data.title: Stitch Fix, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Stitch Fix, Inc., a leading online personal styling service, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of fiscal year 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, highlighting key metrics and business activities. This summary delves into the financial highlights and business highlights as reported by Stitch Fix, Inc.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue, net: $342.1 million, representing a year-over-year increase of 7.3% due to higher average order values and the number of items kept per Fix.
  • Gross Profit: $149.3 million, with a gross margin of 43.6%, a decrease of 180 basis points compared to the prior year due to lower product margins and higher transportation costs.
  • Operating loss: $(8.6) million, a slight improvement from the prior year's operating loss of $(9.0) million.
  • Net loss from continuing operations: $(6.4) million, compared to a net loss of $(6.3) million in the prior year, reflecting a 1.6% increase.
  • Loss per share from continuing operations attributable to common stockholders - Basic and Diluted: $(0.05), consistent with the prior year.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Stitch Fix generates revenue from two primary offerings: 'Fix', a curated shipment of items, and 'Freestyle', direct purchases from their website or app. The company leverages AI and data science to enhance these offerings, which are tailored to individual client preferences.
  • Geographical Performance: The company ceased operations in the UK during the first quarter of fiscal 2024, and the UK business is now reported as a discontinued operation. The focus remains on the U.S. market, where operations continue.
  • Sales Units: As of November 1, 2025, Stitch Fix had approximately 2,307,000 active clients, a decrease from 2,434,000 active clients as of November 2, 2024. This decline is attributed to challenges in client acquisition and retention.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates that macroeconomic uncertainty may impact consumer discretionary spending in fiscal 2026. However, they expect positive trends in average order values and the number of items kept per Fix to offset the impact of lower active client counts on net revenue. Stitch Fix remains focused on retaining current clients, attracting new ones, and enhancing the overall client experience.

SEC Filing: Stitch Fix, Inc. [ SFIX ] - 10-Q - Dec. 05, 2025

data.title: ChargePoint Holdings, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
ChargePoint Holdings, Inc., a leading provider of electric vehicle (EV) charging networks, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of fiscal year 2025. The report highlights significant financial and operational achievements, reflecting the company's ongoing growth and strategic initiatives in the EV infrastructure market.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: ChargePoint reported total revenue of $105.7 million for the three months ended October 31, 2025, an increase from $99.6 million in the same period in 2024. This growth was driven by the expansion of Networked Charging Systems and Subscriptions.

Gross Profit: The company achieved a gross profit of $32.5 million for the three months ended October 31, 2025, up from $22.8 million in the same period in 2024, indicating improved cost management and operational efficiency.

Loss from Operations: ChargePoint reduced its loss from operations to $(44.3) million for the three months ended October 31, 2025, compared to $(68.2) million in the same period in 2024, showing a significant reduction in operating losses.

Net Loss: The net loss for the three months ended October 31, 2025, was $(52.5) million, compared to $(77.6) million in the same period in 2024, reflecting a decrease in net losses.

Net Loss Per Share - Basic and Diluted: The net loss per share was $(2.23) for the three months ended October 31, 2025, compared to $(3.56) in the same period in 2024, indicating an improvement in per-share loss.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: ChargePoint generates revenue primarily through the sale of Networked Charging Systems, subscriptions to the ChargePoint Platform, and extended parts and labor warranties (Assure). The company also offers a bundled service called ChargePoint as a Service (CPaaS), which combines the use of its owned and operated Networked Charging Systems with the ChargePoint Platform and Assure into a single subscription.

Geographical Performance: The United States remains a significant market for ChargePoint, contributing $81.2 million in revenue for the three months ended October 31, 2025, compared to $69.8 million in the same period in 2024. The rest of the world contributed $24.5 million, down from $29.8 million in the previous year, indicating a stronger performance in the domestic market compared to international markets.

Customer Segments: ChargePoint targets three key verticals: commercial, fleet, and residential. Commercial customers include workplaces, retail, hospitality, healthcare, and parking lot operators. The fleet segment includes municipal buses, delivery vehicles, and ride-sharing services, while the residential segment covers single-family homes and multifamily residences.

Future Outlook: ChargePoint anticipates continued growth in its Networked Charging Systems and subscription services as the adoption of electric vehicles increases. The company is focused on expanding its market presence across its key verticals and enhancing its platform capabilities to support the growing demand for EV infrastructure.

SEC Filing: ChargePoint Holdings, Inc. [ CHPT ] - 10-Q - Dec. 05, 2025
